title: CCPA/CPRA — Cross-Context Behavioral Advertising — Cross-Site ID Sync Pixel After GPC Signal

compliance.assurance_rationale: CCPA/CPRA §1798.135 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CCPA/CPRA §1798.135 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.4.21: CCPA/CPRA — Cross-Context Behavioral Advertising — Cross-Site ID Sync Pixel After GPC Signal.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.4.21' running on sourcetype cmp:gpc and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Server-side advertising telemetry HEC, CDN logs
spl:
index=web sourcetype="cmp:gpc" gpc=1 earliest=-7d
| eval gpc_time=_time
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=web sourcetype="ads:sync" cross_context_flag=true earliest=-7d
    | fields session_id, partner_id, _time
  ]
| where _time>=gpc_time
| stats count by partner_id, session_id
| sort - count
description: Global Privacy Control obligates downstream behavior for covered businesses. Detecting third-party sync pixels or bid requests carrying cross-context identifiers after a GPC-positive session start evidences broken CMP / tag enforcement for “sale/share” pathways.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes web and sourcetypes ads:sync, cmp:gpc. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ensure `gpc_time` uses same clock as ad logs; (2) maintain list of `partner_id` considered cross-context; (3) integrate with tag monitoring remediation; (4) legal review for non-California visitors via geo enrichment optional; (5) document CMP version in release notes. Indexes required: `index=web`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ads:sync`, `sourcetype=cmp:gpc`.
knownFalsePositives: Access requests from authorized regulatory inquiries, scheduled subject right campaigns, or outreach during privacy reviews.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Server-side advertising telemetry HEC, CDN logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=web` `sourcetype="cmp:gpc"` (session_id, gpc, _time); `index=web` `sourcetype="ads:sync"` (session_id, partner_id, _time, cross_context_flag).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure `gpc_time` uses same clock as ad logs; (2) maintain list of `partner_id` considered cross-context; (3) integrate with tag monitoring remediation; (4) legal review for non-California visitors via geo enrichment optional; (5) document CMP version in release notes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=web sourcetype="cmp:gpc" gpc=1 earliest=-7d
| eval gpc_time=_time
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=web sourcetype="ads:sync" cross_context_flag=true earliest=-7d
    | fields session_id, partner_id, _time
  ]
| where _time>=gpc_time
| stats count by partner_id, session_id
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA/CPRA — Cross-Context Behavioral Advertising — Cross-Site ID Sync Pixel After GPC Signal** — Global Privacy Control obligates downstream behavior for covered businesses. Detecting third-party sync pixels or bid requests carrying cross-context identifiers after a GPC-positive session start evidences broken CMP / tag enforcement for “sale/share” pathways.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=web` `sourcetype="cmp:gpc"` (session_id, gpc, _time); `index=web` `sourcetype="ads:sync"` (session_id, partner_id, _time, cross_context_flag). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Server-side advertising telemetry HEC, CDN logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: web; **sourcetype**: cmp:gpc. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=web, sourcetype="cmp:gpc", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gpc_time** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where _time>=gpc_time` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **partner_id, session_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: gpc_time, partner_id, session_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (partner_id), Bar chart, Single value (sessions).
